Marisa Allen.review_text: It is literally just attending a lecture and doing a short quiz every week. Super easy but I wish I knew it was not required. It's nice listening to the lectures but added to stress by having mandatory attendance. She is very sweet nonetheless. If you can afford going to the lecture every week, why not? But also no need for this class.

Jeff Welter.review_text: Love this dude! It's a hard class but he made it enjoyable. There are a lot of guest speakers (some are very boring) and the info on the lecture slides are exactly what's on the test. Tests are very hard so make sure you study and go over the lecture slides.

Jeff is great professor. He makes sure the material is easy and fun to learn and makes it so the material is easier to learn in other business courses. Most of your grade depends on 2 exams, but they're easy if you pay attention and revisit the slides (no textbook!). Guest lecturers usually come in, but Jeff is easily the best one.
Professor Welter's class is informative and an easy A. The only thing that some might find annoying is that attendance is the biggest part of your grade. Other than that there is close to no hw.
Super easy but helpful class. Virtually no work to do, with an easy open note quiz once a week. No tests, no papers, just informative classes.

Super easy class, learned a lot about different business fields. You will have to buy a clicker which is used for attendance in class. The work was literally one quiz per week based on the class lecture. Overall easy class and nice professor.

Jeff is great and so is the class. The class is simple but extremely informative and interesting. Honestly, this class was my favorite one this past semester. 1 quiz/week but the answers are on the slides he provides every week. There's also graded clicker participation. Easy A.

Simple class, but helpful. Really introduces you to basic business concepts. One quiz a week, clicker participation, and one career quest outside of class. All of the quizzes are based off of the lecture for the week. Pretty much impossible to do bad. Jeff doesn't teach the whole semester, so expect a lot of guest lecturers. Still pretty easy!

Super easy and straightforward class, great professor. Mostly graded by attendance. All you have to do is show up to class (once a week), and answer 5 questions in class (it doesn't matter if you answer correctly). You can skip 3 classes out of the 13 total and still get full credit for attendance. There is one quiz a week but it is open note.
He is a very good professor! He helped me a lot and guide me. I would like to take his class again!!!

He's only in class a couple of times during the semester because of all the guest speakers, but he's pretty cool. The lectures that he spoke in were kind of fun. Class is really easy. One 5-question quiz per week and only meets once a week. Two exams, but they are easy.

This class is pretty easy for the most part, especially since you only meet once a week. You will need a clicker and participation is mandatory. There are weekly easy quizzes, one career leadership meeting outside of class, one midterm, and a final. The exams are pretty tough and the course is heavily weighed on that so study!
Jeff Welter is an awesome professor and is super passionate about the subject. He cares about his students and is very willing to help. Such an easy class, super self-explanatory.

Welter is a charismatic lecturer that always prepares clear and easy to understand powerpoints. Though your grade is based on two exams, info is based on the powerpoints. Since this class isn't as difficult as others, it can be fun and you learn a few things that will benefit you in future business classes and applications.

Super easy class. The tests are worth a big percent of your grade, but are based entirely off of the powerpoints from the lectures. As long as you show up to class and study for the two tests, you should get an A.
Great class! Tests are super easy just look through the slides! Jeff is so sweet and a phenomenal lecturer. I wish he taught more classes because I would take them all.
Nice guy but your grade relies on two exams that his class does not prepare you for

I loved the lectures and actually learned a lot. However, it is difficult to get an A because the grade is based on 2 exams and you can only lose 35 points to get an A. So if you don't do well in one of the exams you won't be able to get an A because there's no extra credit. I don't think its fair because my gpa did go down over a 1 credit course.

The exams are hard, you have to memorize all the lecture thigs, I got a 90 on the first one but can't even get an 80 on the second exam! This one-credit course shouldn't be that hard, the statistics show less than 20% got 80+ in the exam, that's funny
